content: We can hypothesize that the intrinsic curiosity signals generated by ICM/RND frameworks can be used to dynamically prioritize which new interaction experiences an active learning agent stores in its memory buffer, rather than storing all encountered data indiscriminately. This would let the agent grow its memory only with high-surprise, learning-driving experiences that align with its natural exploratory drive, cutting down redundant memory storage and accelerating long-run task performance.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
